## Protein 2

**NCBI_entry**: [MBE6593352.1](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/protein/MBE6593352.1)

The second protein analyzed is annotated as a hypothetical protein from an Oscillospiraceae family bacterium. 
This protein sequence comes from a Metagenome-Assembled Genome (MAG) not yet characterized from a metagenomic study of goat gut microbiomes.

This anaerobic bacterial group is abundant in herbivore gut microbiomes, where they are key contributors to lignocellulose degradation and fermentation processes, contributing to the production of short-chain fatty acids that are essential for host metabolism @peng2021. 
Metagenomic studies indicate that a large proportion of  proteins of Oscillospiraceae family remain uncharacterized [@peng2021, @gophna2017], which explains the annotation of this sequence as a hypothetical protein.

Proteins are annotated as hypothetical when an open reading frame is found through computational gene prediction (like in MAGs), but no experimental evidence or significant similarity to proteins with known function is available for the the protein [@naveed2018; @sivashankari2006].

The specific function of this protein remains unknown. However, as metagenomic studies suggest it participates in carbohydrate metabolism and fermentation-related pathways @peng2021, characterizing this protein might provide information about new enzymatic functions associated with microbial biomass degradation.

In addition, the lack of structural data in both the PDB and AlphaFold databases highlights the importance of computational modelling approaches to predict its three-dimensional structure and infer potential functional features.
